name: Naive Bayes
description: Naive Bayes is a probabilistic classification algorithm based on Bayes’ theorem. It is commonly used for text classification, document categorization, and other scenarios involving discrete or binary features.

fields.name: featuresCol
fields.title: Features Column
fields.description: Vector column containing non-negative feature values used to estimate class probabilities.

fields.name: labelCol
fields.title: Label Column
fields.description: Target column representing the actual class or category the model is trained to predict.

fields.name: predictionCol
fields.title: Prediction Column
fields.description: Name of the column where predicted class labels are written after model scoring.

fields.name: splitRatio
fields.title: Split Ratio
fields.description: Controls how the dataset is divided between training and validation for performance evaluation.

fields.name: path
fields.title: Path
fields.description: File system location where the confusion matrix output is stored for reporting or auditing.

fields.name: modelType
fields.title: modelType
fields.description: Defines the Naive Bayes variant used for training. Multinomial is suited for count-based data, while Bernoulli is suited for binary features.

fields.name: smoothing
fields.title: Smoothing
fields.description: Smoothing parameter used to handle zero-frequency problems and improve probability estimation stability.

fields.name: weightCol
fields.title: Weight Column
fields.description: Optional column specifying the relative importance of records during model training.
